steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states Nazareth's belief: Elvis's music is best understood as an anthology showcasing many artists' contributions. The blank connects that belief to his action of naming his college course "Elvis as Anthology." The transition must show the course title was a deliberate result of that belief.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In addition,': signals a new, separate point is being added. The course title is not a separate point — it is the direct application of the stated belief. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In sum,': signals a summary or conclusion of multiple prior points. There is only one prior point, and the second clause is an action, not a wrap-up. ✗
  • Choice C — 'That is,': signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The course title is not a rephrasing of the belief; it is something Nazareth did in response to it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To that end,': signals that the following action was taken in order to achieve or act on the stated goal or belief. Naming the course "Elvis as Anthology" was precisely Nazareth putting his belief into practice. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
